question: How many patients do we have?
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_patients FROM patients;

question: List all doctors and their specializations
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query: SELECT name, specialization FROM doctors ORDER BY name;

raw_response.sql_query: SELECT name, specialization FROM doctors ORDER BY name;

question: Show me appointments for last month
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT id, patient_id, doctor_id, appointment_date, status
                    FROM appointments
                    WHERE appointment_date >= date('now', 'start of month', '-1 month')
                      AND appointment_date < date('now', 'start of month')
                    ORDER BY appointment_date;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT id, patient_id, doctor_id, appointment_date, status
                    FROM appointments
                    WHERE appointment_date >= date('now', 'start of month', '-1 month')
                      AND appointment_date < date('now', 'start of month')
                    ORDER BY appointment_date;

question: Which doctor has the most appointments?
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
                    FROM appointments a
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.id, d.name
                    ORDER BY appointment_count DESC
                    LIMIT 1;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
                    FROM appointments a
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.id, d.name
                    ORDER BY appointment_count DESC
                    LIMIT 1;

question: What is the total revenue?
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM invoices;

raw_response.sql_query: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM invoices;

question: Show revenue by doctor
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT d.name,
                           SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_revenue
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN appointments a ON i.patient_id = a.patient_id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.id, d.name
                    ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT d.name,
                           SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_revenue
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN appointments a ON i.patient_id = a.patient_id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.id, d.name
                    ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: How many cancelled appointments last quarter?
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cancelled_count
                    FROM appointments
                    WHERE status = 'Cancelled'
                      AND appointment_date >= date('now', '-3 months');

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cancelled_count
                    FROM appointments
                    WHERE status = 'Cancelled'
                      AND appointment_date >= date('now', '-3 months');

question: Top 5 patients by spending
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT p.first_name,
                           p.last_name,
                           SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_spending
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN patients p ON i.patient_id = p.id
                    GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
                    ORDER BY total_spending DESC
                    LIMIT 5;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT p.first_name,
                           p.last_name,
                           SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_spending
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN patients p ON i.patient_id = p.id
                    GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
                    ORDER BY total_spending DESC
                    LIMIT 5;

question: Average treatment cost by specialization
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT d.specialization,
                           AVG(t.cost) AS avg_treatment_cost
                    FROM treatments t
                    JOIN appointments a ON t.appointment_id = a.id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.specialization
                    ORDER BY avg_treatment_cost DESC;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT d.specialization,
                           AVG(t.cost) AS avg_treatment_cost
                    FROM treatments t
                    JOIN appointments a ON t.appointment_id = a.id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.specialization
                    ORDER BY avg_treatment_cost DESC;

question: Show monthly appointment count for the past 6 months
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', appointment_date) AS month,
                           COUNT(*) AS total_appointments
                    FROM appointments
                    WHERE appointment_date >= date('now', '-6 months')
                    GROUP BY month
                    ORDER BY month;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', appointment_date) AS month,
                           COUNT(*) AS total_appointments
                    FROM appointments
                    WHERE appointment_date >= date('now', '-6 months')
                    GROUP BY month
                    ORDER BY month;

question: Which city has the most patients?
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS patient_count
                    FROM patients
                    GROUP BY city
                    ORDER BY patient_count DESC
                    LIMIT 1;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS patient_count
                    FROM patients
                    GROUP BY city
                    ORDER BY patient_count DESC
                    LIMIT 1;

question: List patients who visited more than 3 times
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT p.first_name,
                           p.last_name,
                           COUNT(a.id) AS visit_count
                    FROM appointments a
                    JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.id
                    GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
                    HAVING COUNT(a.id) > 3
                    ORDER BY visit_count DESC;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT p.first_name,
                           p.last_name,
                           COUNT(a.id) AS visit_count
                    FROM appointments a
                    JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.id
                    GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
                    HAVING COUNT(a.id) > 3
                    ORDER BY visit_count DESC;

question: Show unpaid invoices
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT id, patient_id, invoice_date, total_amount, paid_amount, status
                    FROM invoices
                    WHERE status IN ('Pending', 'Overdue')
                    ORDER BY invoice_date DESC;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT id, patient_id, invoice_date, total_amount, paid_amount, status
                    FROM invoices
                    WHERE status IN ('Pending', 'Overdue')
                    ORDER BY invoice_date DESC;

question: What percentage of appointments are no-shows?
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows

question: Show the busiest day of the week for appointments
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT CASE strftime('%w', appointment_date)
                               WHEN '0' THEN 'Sunday'
                               WHEN '1' THEN 'Monday'
                               WHEN '2' THEN 'Tuesday'
                               WHEN '3' THEN 'Wednesday'
                               WHEN '4' THEN 'Thursday'
                               WHEN '5' THEN 'Friday'
                               WHEN '6' THEN 'Saturday'
                           END AS weekday,
                           COUNT(*) AS total_appointments
                    FROM appointments
                    GROUP BY weekday
                    ORDER BY total_appointments DESC
                    LIMIT 1;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT CASE strftime('%w', appointment_date)
                               WHEN '0' THEN 'Sunday'
                               WHEN '1' THEN 'Monday'
                               WHEN '2' THEN 'Tuesday'
                               WHEN '3' THEN 'Wednesday'
                               WHEN '4' THEN 'Thursday'
                               WHEN '5' THEN 'Friday'
                               WHEN '6' THEN 'Saturday'
                           END AS weekday,
                           COUNT(*) AS total_appointments
                    FROM appointments
                    GROUP BY weekday
                    ORDER BY total_appointments DESC
                    LIMIT 1;

                    GROUP BY month

question: Average appointment duration by doctor
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT d.name,
                           AVG(t.duration_minutes) AS avg_duration_minutes
                    FROM treatments t
                    JOIN appointments a ON t.appointment_id = a.id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.id, d.name
                    ORDER BY avg_duration_minutes DESC;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT d.name,
                           AVG(t.duration_minutes) AS avg_duration_minutes
                    FROM treatments t
                    JOIN appointments a ON t.appointment_id = a.id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.id, d.name
                    ORDER BY avg_duration_minutes DESC;

question: List patients with overdue invoices
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT DISTINCT p.first_name, p.last_name, p.city
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN patients p ON i.patient_id = p.id
                    WHERE i.status = 'Overdue'
                    ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT DISTINCT p.first_name, p.last_name, p.city
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN patients p ON i.patient_id = p.id
                    WHERE i.status = 'Overdue'
                    ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name;

question: Compare revenue between departments
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT d.department,
                           SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_revenue
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN appointments a ON i.patient_id = a.patient_id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.department
                    ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT d.department,
                           SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_revenue
                    FROM invoices i
                    JOIN appointments a ON i.patient_id = a.patient_id
                    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.id
                    GROUP BY d.department
                    ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: Show patient registration trend by month
notes: SQL executed successfully and returned rows
sql_query:
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', registered_date) AS month,
                           COUNT(*) AS registrations
                    FROM patients
                    GROUP BY month
                    ORDER BY month;

raw_response.sql_query:
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', registered_date) AS month,
                           COUNT(*) AS registrations
                    FROM patients
                    GROUP BY month
                    ORDER BY month;
